title: NOC 72021 – Contractors and supervisors, heavy equipment operator crews | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Contractors and Supervisors of Heavy Equipment Operator Crews (NOC 72021) are the frontline leaders driving Canada’s massive civil engineering and resource extraction projects. As the federal government pushes for rapid housing infrastructure and expanded mining for critical minerals, these supervisors are responsible for coordinating the heavy machinery that shapes the landscape. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they manage crews of crane operators, drillers, and heavy equipment operators, ensuring that multi-million dollar fleets are utilized efficiently and safely under the strict environmental regulations of 2026.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Crew Coordination: Supervising, coordinating, and scheduling the activities of workers who operate cranes and heavy construction, paving, and drilling equipment.
Operational Strategy: Establishing methods to meet work schedules and coordinating activities with other project managers and sub-trades.
Resource Logistics: Requisitioning materials and supplies and ensuring that all heavy machinery is maintained to 2026 safety and emission standards.
Training & Safety: Mentoring operators on new equipment technologies and ensuring strict compliance with Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) regulations on site.
Reporting: Preparing production, safety, and equipment-usage reports and recommending personnel actions such as hirings or promotions.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson in the occupation being supervised (e.g., Heavy Equipment Operator or Crane Operator) are mandatory.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in a relevant trade may be required depending on the province and specific equipment (e.g., Crane Operators).
Leadership Skills: Proficiency in Fleet Management Software and digital site-mapping tools.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, particularly in provinces with active large-scale infrastructure and mining sectors:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On February 18, 2026, Ontario issued a targeted OINP draw specifically for NOC 72021 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was 50, highlighting a critical need for supervisors to lead road, bridge, and transit projects across the province.
Alberta: High Demand. Alberta offers the highest median wages for this NOC at $41.55/hr, with top earners in the oil sands and heavy construction reaching upwards of $80.00/hr.

British Columbia: Good prospects. Demand is high in the "Mainland/Southwest" and "Thompson-Okanagan" regions for pipeline and highway expansion. BC projects over 1,400 openings in related heavy equipment sectors through 2035.

National Trend: Over 70% of the workforce is concentrated in the Construction sector. With significant retirements expected (over 500 projected openings over the next decade in Atlantic Canada alone), the "leadership gap" is a major driver for international recruitment.
Why Work in Canada as a Heavy Equipment Supervisor?

Lead Large-Scale Infrastructure: Direct high-impact projects like the Ontario Line subway or Alberta’s energy corridor expansions using 2026-grade GPS-guided grading and automated fleet management systems.
Top-Tier Trades Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential is among the highest in the trades, with national median annual earnings around $96,100.
Entrepreneurial Freedom: A significant portion of this NOC includes Contractors who own and operate their own heavy equipment rental or site-preparation firms.

Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent highlight in Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) draws and is a primary target for the 2026 Express Entry "Transport and Construction" categories.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On February 18, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72021 under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Transport & Trades Categories): IRCC has prioritized "Transport and Construction Occupations" for category-based draws in 2026, allowing supervisors with 1 year of experience to receive invitations with lower CRS scores.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly confirmed federal initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents with skilled trade experience. Heavy equipment supervisors are classified as "High-Priority Construction Workers."
BC PNP Construction Stream: A reliable route for supervisors with a job offer from a BC-based heavy construction or forestry firm.
